name: Velvet Corn Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Cooked Smithfield (or Hickory-smoked) ham, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the corn in the stock and drain it. Reserve the stock, chop the corn finely, and return it to the stock.
2. In a small bowl, mix cornstarch with cold stock to make a paste.
3. Heat oil in a heavy 2-quart saucepan until hot. Saute green onions and ginger for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly to avoid burning.
4. Add stock and corn to the saucepan; stir and bring to just under a boil.
5. Add salt, pepper, and sugar. Simmer for 15 minutes or until ready to serve.
6. Just before serving, turn up the heat to a gentle simmer. Dribble in the cornstarch paste until the soup acquires a waxy translucence. It should still be thin but not watery.
7. Add sherry.
8. Beat egg whites with a fork to a light froth. Turn off the heat.
9. Quickly swirl in the egg whites and pour into a serving bowl. Garnish with ham. Serve.
